title: eriogonum alatum var. elatum
intro: Eriogonum alatum var. elatum, also known as California buckwheat or Mojave buckwheat, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe and protect the skin. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing. Eriogonum alatum var. elatum can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
